etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, "tree") + phonetic 反(反) (fǎn) -> board; plank
etymology.interpretation: The character 板 (bǎn, "board; plank") is a phono-semantic compound. The semantic 木(木) (mù, "tree") indicates the material of wood, which is the substance of a board. The phonetic 反(反) (fǎn) provides the pronunciation. This character directly refers to objects made from wood that are flat, like boards or planks.

examples.chinese: 他用力把钉子钉进木板(他用力把釘子釘進木板)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yònglì bǎ dīngzi dīng jìn mùbǎn.
examples.english: He forcefully nailed the nail into the wooden board.

examples.chinese: 制作这个模型需要许多木板(製作這個模型需要許多木板)。
examples.pinyin: Zhìzuò zhège móxíng xūyào xǔduō mùbǎn.
examples.english: Many wooden planks are needed to build this model.

examples.chinese: 老师在黑板上写下了答案(老師在黑板上寫下了答案)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī zài hēibǎn shàng xiěxià le dá'àn.
examples.english: The teacher wrote the answers on the blackboard.
